rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GENETTE TRAMMELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Donald Trammell By And Through His Gal Clinton Killians Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Keystone Law Group P.C.S Special Motion To Strike C.C.P. Section 425.16 Second Amended Petition Under Probate Code Sections 850, 859.
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative is issued by Hon. Joseph M. Quinn The Court GRANTS Petitioner and Moving Party Donald Trammell's request for judicial notice. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Trammell's request to continue the hearing on this motion to a date after the order on the Petition for Probate of Lost Will has been entered is DENIED. Before the court is Trammell's Motion for Reconsideration of Order Granting Keystone Law Group P.C.'s Special Motion to Strike (C.C.P. section 425.16) Second Amended Petition Under Probate Code Sections 850, 859 ("Reconsideration Motion"). Trammell asks the court to "amend" its April 9, 2021 Order granting Keystone's anti-SLAPP motion ("April 9 Anti-SLAPP Order"), such that the order is "denied in part or whole, or in the alternative, [the motion is ordered] rebriefed for the court to consider the new and different facts and law regarding the case." (Mot. at p. 1.) Trammell's Reconsideration Motion is DENIED. Under subdivision (a) of section 1008 of the Code of Civil Procedure, a party affected by an order may bring a motion for reconsideration within 10 days of service of notice of entry of the order, "and based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law, make application to the same judge or court that made the order, to reconsider the matter and modify, amend, or revoke the prior order." The moving party must not only demonstrate the grounds for reconsideration, but also "show diligence with a satisfactory explanation for not presenting the new or different information earlier." (Even Zohar Constr. & Remodeling, Inc. v. Bellaire Townhouses, LLC (2015) 61 Cal.4th 830, 833.) The moving party may not simply resubmit facts, circumstances, or law previously raised in the first motion and call them "new or different." (See Kerns v. CSE Ins. Grp. (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 368, 383.) "The party making the application," the Legislature directs, "shall state by affidavit what application was made before, when and to what judge, what order or decisions were made, and what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." (See Code Civ. Proc., section 1008, subd. (a).) If the statutory requirements for a motion for reconsideration are not met, the motion may be denied on those grounds. "A trial court must exercise due consideration before modifying, amending, or revoking its prior order." (Case v. Lazben Fin. Co. (2002) 99 Cal.App.4th 172, 189.) Here, Trammell's counsel, Nader Fannyan, submitted a declaration identifying the April 9 Anti-SLAPP Order and one "new or different fact" warranting reconsideration - the April 7, 2021 Will Contest Judgment in the related will contest ("Will Contest Judgment"), finding decedent Gennette Trammell's 2015 Will is valid. Notably, another judge entered the Will Context Judgment after a trial on that petition. The court rejected a claim that decedent lacked testamentary capacity in 2015 and found the decedent's 2015 will was "properly executed, correct, and valid" and, by its express language, revoked the Trust and Pour Over Will decedent executed on February 28, 2004.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GENETTE TRAMMELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Respondent Keystone Law Group?S Motion To Strike Ccp Section 425.16 Second Amended Petition Under Probate Code Sections 850, 859
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] The Will Contest Judgment is a new fact, but it does not support an order modifying, amending or revoking the April 9 Anti-SLAPP Order. The Will Contest Judgment does not undermine the court's finding that Trammell's allegations and claims against Keystone arise from activity protected under section 425.16. In the April 9 Order the court found that at the center of Trammell's claim against Keystone is Keystone's advocacy and, therefore, the claims arose from protected activity. (See April 9 Anti-SLAPP Order at pp. 5-7.) The Will Contest Judgment says nothing about the nature of Trammell's claim against Keystone. Trammell's assertion that the Will Contest Judgment could support new claims and legal theories against Keystone is beside the point. The April 9 Anti-SLAPP order addresses Trammell's claim against Keystone that is set out in the operative petition; other claims that Trammell may have against Keystone are irrelevant. Nor does the Will Contest Judgment undermine the court's finding that Trammell failed to show a probability of prevailing on his claim against Keystone. In the April 9 Order, the court found the record devoid of evidence to support Trammel's claim against Keystone. (See April 9 Anti-SLAPP Order at pp. 6-7.) Indeed, the evidence on the anti-SLAPP motion demonstrated that Keystone's advocacy was in good faith and that Keystone did not know of any wrongdoing by Porchia. (Id. at p. 9.) The Will Contest Judgment-which focused on decedent's testamentary capacity in 2015-says nothing about Keystone's advocacy and makes no findings regarding Keystone's knowledge regarding the 2015 Will and its relationship to the 2004 Trust. Trammell's argument that new facts support a finding that Keystone's anti-SLAPP motion was untimely is not properly before the court because Mr. Fannyan's declaration did not identify new facts that support the argument. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 1008, subd. (a).) In any event, the motion on these grounds fails because Trammell has not demonstrated why the "new" evidence-an email that counsel has had in his possession for over a year-was not presented to the court in the opposition to the anti-SLAPP motion. Finally, this claim fails on the merits. The "new" email shows only that Keystone agreed to accept service of the First Amended Petition in March 2020. It does not show that service was made or accepted. Moreover, the email conversation in October 2020-which was before the court when it issued the anti-SLAPP ruling-shows that service was not accepted in March and was only accepted in October. Indeed, Keystone made no general appearance in these proceedings prior to October 2020. Trammell has not met his burden under section 1008 for reconsideration on this basis. Keystone is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on May 25, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. The appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/23/2021. Third continuance for the same reason. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Order must being with Order For Final Distribution. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/7/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROSABELLA SAFONT TRUST

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/26/21 per stipulation of counsel.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MADGE MARIE ULMAN 2004 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For (1)Determination That The Amendment To The Madge Marie Ulman 2004 Revocable Trust Is Void And To Impose A Constructive Trust
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Court to determine whether venue is proper in San Francisco. 2) The Petition names Betty Regan and Does 1 through 100, but Betty Regan is deceased and the Does remain unnamed. Petitioner states in the supplemental declaration filed on 5/4/21 that the present respondents are Wells Fargo Bank and Does 1 through 100, but an amendment to the petition has not been filed. It is also unclear why Wells Fargo is named as a respondent as no allegations have been made against it. In addition, the following procedural issues are noted: 1) Notice of Hearing filed on 5/3/21 shows that Kaye Marshall was provided notice; however, the notice was not 30 days prior to the hearing date listed. In addition, his address has not been included in the petition. 2) Faye Marie Hill's address has not been identified and she has not been given notice. 3) Objection filed on 3/19/21 alleges corrected identities and parties, which must be addressed by Petitioner and corrected, if needed, and new notices provided. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 9th amended general order.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHUEN MOON LAU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/14/2021 1. Please explain the reasons for Donny H. Lau being an interpreter/translator for Decedent's Will. Please explain Decedent's understanding of English/the Will and why a interpreter/translator was needed. 2. In addition to all persons mentioned in the will, all heirs must be listed even if a decedent died testate. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C]. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/1/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YINYIU CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/7/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] No proof of service of notice. No proof of publication. No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A.] No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. The petition caption is incomplete if full IAEA authority is being sought. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/25/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF XIU CHAN MEI RUAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/7/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Not all heirs have waived bond. Item 5b of the petition must be answered. One of the grandchildren is a minor. His age must be set forth in a supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the petitioner. The person having legal custody of the minor, with whom the minor resides, is also missing from item 8 of the petition. That person must also be given. [CRC 7.51(d).] The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/25/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
